headline: Will Berkshire’s Domino’s (DPZ) Exit and Buybacks Shift the Chain’s Capital Allocation Narrative?
summary: Earlier this year, Domino’s Pizza reported softer first-quarter 2026 results, trimmed its U.S. same-store sales guidance to low single digits, and announced a US$1.00 billion share repurchase plan alongside intentions to open over 175 net new U.S. stores in 2026. At the same time, Berkshire Hathaway fully exited its near-10% Domino’s stake while Domino’s launched high-profile soccer-themed promotions, including up to US$1.00 million in free “Emergency Pizzas” and gaming tie-ins, sharpening...
